Nio Onvo targets over 3,300 available swap stations by year-end
articles.summary: Nio's sub-brand Onvo aims to expand its available battery swap stations to over 3,300 by the end of the year, up from 2,491 currently, to support its intensive new product roll-out. This expansion includes the launch of new L80 and revamped L60 models. The company is also developing fifth-generation swap stations that will support vehicles from another sub-brand, Firefly, with large-scale deployment starting in July and August.

articles.title: Pass It On Outdoor Mentors Awarded Grant from Wisconsin Alliant Energy
articles.summary: Pass It On – Outdoor Mentors has received a grant from Alliant Energy in Wisconsin. This funding will support connecting Wisconsin youth with mentors for outdoor activities like hunting, fishing, and conservation, helping to build confidence and life skills while fostering the next generation of conservationists. Both organizations emphasize the importance of these experiences for youth who may not otherwise have access to the outdoors.

articles.title: LA500 2026: Ted Sarandos
articles.summary: Ted Sarandos, co-chief executive of Netflix, has been recognized in the LA500 for his leadership in content operations since 2000 and his pivotal role in transitioning Netflix to original content production. This entry marks his eleventh year on the LA500 list from the Los Angeles Business Journal.

articles.title: Private markets face retail reset as firms push for greater transparency
articles.summary: Private markets are undergoing a significant shift as individual investors become more cautious, prompting firms to enhance transparency. Fundraising for private equity and private credit has slowed, particularly among retail investors, despite KKR's survey suggesting continued demand for private market concepts. In response, Apollo is exploring selling a private credit fund and plans to introduce daily pricing for its credit funds, while StepStone Group and PitchBook are partnering to offer more detailed deal-level benchmarks.
articles.content: Private markets are undergoing a significant shift as individual investors become more cautious, prompting firms to enhance transparency. Fundraising for private equity and private credit has slowed, particularly among retail investors, despite KKR's survey suggesting continued demand for private market concepts. In response, Apollo is exploring selling a private credit fund and plans to introduce daily pricing for its credit funds, while StepStone Group and PitchBook are partnering to offer more detailed deal-level benchmarks.

articles.summary: Nordson Corporation (US6556631025) stock trades at $283.53, having gained 17.9% year-to-date. Brokerages have set a consensus price target of $311.50 for the precision dispensing equipment leader, indicating potential upside. The company is set to release its Q2 earnings on May 20, and investors are closely watching its performance within the industrial sector.

articles.title: Entergy Corp. stock (US29364G1031): Citi raises price target after Q1 earnings
articles.summary: Citi analysts have raised their price target for Entergy Corp. (ETR) to $121 from $116 despite the utility company missing Q1 2026 earnings estimates with an EPS of $0.86 and revenue of $2.96 billion. Entergy, which serves millions in the U.S. Southeast, maintains a Neutral rating from Citi and focuses on grid modernization and clean energy transitions with a significant capital expenditure plan. The stock opened at $111.85, up 0.2% on May 11, and offers a strong dividend profile appealing to income-focused investors.

articles.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on McDonald's to $305 From $325
articles.summary: JPMorgan has revised its price target for McDonald's (NYSE: MCD) to $305, down from the previous $325. This adjustment was reported by MT Newswires on May 11, 2026. This comes amidst other analysts also adjusting their price targets for McDonald's recently.

articles.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on TransDigm to $1,575 From $1,565
articles.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for TransDigm Group Inc. (TDG) to $1,575 from $1,565, as reported by MT Newswires on May 11, 2026. This adjustment comes amidst other recent analyst revisions for the aerospace and defense company. The article also provides a company profile, financial data, and analyst consensus information for TransDigm.

articles.title: Manning & Napier Advisors LLC Sells 3,121 Shares of Deere & Company $DE
articles.summary: Manning & Napier Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in Deere & Company, selling 3,121 shares in the fourth quarter, decreasing its holdings by 63.0% to 1,833 shares valued at approximately $861,000. Despite this reduction, Deere & Company continues to receive bullish analyst attention with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and strong fundamentals, including recent EPS and revenue beats. Other institutional investors have adjusted their positions, mostly increasing their holdings, while analysts predict an average target price of $655.45 for the stock.

articles.title: MGO One Seven LLC Reduces Position in Public Storage $PSA
articles.summary: MGO One Seven LLC significantly reduced its stake in Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) by 28.3% in the fourth quarter of the previous year, selling 3,463 shares and retaining 8,764 shares valued at $2.27 million. Despite this, Public Storage reported strong quarterly earnings, beating EPS estimates and confirming a $3.00 quarterly dividend payment. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock with an average price target of $309.78.
